request.description:
This endpoint allows you to send a template message to WhatsApp phone number

### Request Body

- token (text, required): The token for authentication.
    
- phone (text, required): The phone number of the recipient.s
    
- template_name (text, required): The name of the template to be used.
    
- template_language (text, required): The language of the template to be used.
    
- components (array, required): An array of message components based on the Facebook WhatsApp API [https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/cloud-api/guides/send-message-templates](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/cloud-api/guides/send-message-templates)

This endpoint makes an HTTP GET request to retrieve templates from the API. It requires a token parameter in the query string for authentication.

### Request

- Method: GET
- Endpoint: {{api_endpoint}}api/wpbox/getTemplates
- Query Parameters:
    - token (string, required): The authentication token.

### Response

- Status: 200 OK
- Body:
    
    ``` json
          {
            "status": "",
            "templates": [
              {
                "id": 0,
                "name": "",
                "status": "",
                "category": "",
                "language": "",
                "components": "",
                "company_id": 0,
                "created_at": "",
                "updated_at": ""
              }
            ]
          }
    
     ```
    

The response contains a status field and an array of templates, each with an ID, name, status, category, language, components, company ID, creation timestamp, and last update timestamp.

👉 `https://apps.oncloudapi.com/api/wpbox/sendtemplatemessage`

Below is your **final ready-to-use documentation** for your WhatsApp Cloud API (with PDF sending support) — formatted in Markdown style for GitHub, API docs, or Postman collection use.

---

# 📄 WhatsApp Cloud API — Send Template Message with PDF Document

## 🌐 Endpoint

```
POST https://apps.oncloudapi.com/api/wpbox/sendtemplatemessage

 ```

---

## 🧠 Overview

This API sends **WhatsApp template messages** with an attached **PDF file (Document Header)** using an approved template in your WhatsApp Business account.

You can use this to send invoices, receipts, or reports automatically.

---

## 🧩 Request Example

```
{
  "token": "token",
  "phone": "923354334982",
  "template_name": "pdf_message",
  "template_language": "en",
  "components": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "parameters": [
        {
          "type": "document",
          "document": {
            "link": "https://yourdomain.com/invoice.pdf",
            "filename": "Invoice_Asher.pdf"
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "type": "body",
      "parameters": [
        { "type": "text", "text": "Asher" },
        { "type": "text", "text": "On Cloud APIs" },
        { "type": "text", "text": "PKR 2,500" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

 ```

---

## 🧾 Parameter Details

| Field | Type | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `token` | string | Your API authentication token. | ✅ |
| `phone` | string | WhatsApp number (without `+`, include country code). Example: `923001234567` | ✅ |
| `template_name` | string | Name of the approved WhatsApp message template. | ✅ |
| `template_language` | string | Template language code (e.g., `en`, `en_US`). | ✅ |
| `components` | array | Template components (header, body, buttons, etc.). | ✅ |

---

## 🧱 Component Structure

### 1️⃣ Header (Document)

```
{
  "type": "header",
  "parameters": [
    {
      "type": "document",
      "document": {
        "link": "https://yourdomain.com/invoice.pdf",
        "filename": "Invoice_Asher.pdf"
      }
    }
  ]
}

 ```

- **link** → Direct public URL of your PDF file (must be accessible without login).
    
- **filename** → Optional, defines how the file will be displayed to the user.
    
- Ensure your **template has a “Document” header type** in the WhatsApp Business Manager.
    

---

### 2️⃣ Body (Text Parameters)

```
{
  "type": "body",
  "parameters": [
    { "type": "text", "text": "Asher" },
    { "type": "text", "text": "On Cloud APIs" },
    { "type": "text", "text": "PKR 2,500" }
  ]
}

 ```

Each parameter corresponds to a placeholder in your WhatsApp template, e.g. `{{1}}`, `{{2}}`, `{{3}}`.

---

## 📋 WhatsApp Template Setup Example

**Template Name:** `pdf_message`  
**Language:** `English (en)`  
**Template Structure:**

```
Header: [Document]
Body: Hello {{1}}, your payment for {{2}} of {{3}} has been received.

 ```

---

## ⚙️ Example — PHP cURL Request

```
$url = "https://apps.oncloudapi.com/api/wpbox/sendtemplatemessage";
$data = [
  "token" => "token",
  "phone" => "923354334982",
  "template_name" => "pdf_message",
  "template_language" => "en",
  "components" => [
    [
      "type" => "header",
      "parameters" => [
        [
          "type" => "document",
          "document" => [
            "link" => "https://yourdomain.com/invoice.pdf",
            "filename" => "Invoice_Asher.pdf"
          ]
        ]
      ]
    ],
    [
      "type" => "body",
      "parameters" => [
        ["type" => "text", "text" => "Asher"],
        ["type" => "text", "text" => "On Cloud APIs"],
        ["type" => "text", "text" => "PKR 2,500"]
      ]
    ]
  ]
];
$ch = curl_init($url);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POST, true);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, ['Content-Type: application/json']);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, json_encode($data));
$response = curl_exec($ch);
curl_close($ch);
echo $response;
?>

 ```

---

## ✅ Successful Response Example

```
{
  "success": true,
  "message_id": "wamid.HBgLMjMyMzU0MzM0OTgyFQIAEhgUMzg5NzQ5NkEyQjRBMDlFMzgzMUEA",
  "status": "sent",
  "to": "923354334982"
}

 ```

---

## ⚠️ Error Codes & Troubleshooting

| Error Message | Description | Solution |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Header parameter missing` | Template header not configured for document | Edit your template in Meta → add Document header |
| `Invalid token` | API token expired or invalid | Regenerate your token from OnCloud API dashboard |
| `Invalid URL` | PDF link inaccessible or private | Use a direct public link (e.g., from your hosting or CDN) |
| `Unsupported Media Type` | File type is not PDF | Ensure you upload `.pdf` format only |

---

## 💡 Notes

- The **maximum file size** for PDFs is **100 MB**.
    
- You can reuse the same template for different invoices by changing the body text and document link dynamically.
    
- Make sure your WhatsApp Business Account is approved to send **media templates**.

This endpoint makes an HTTP GET request to retrieve groups from the On Cloud API. It requires a token parameter in the query string. The parameter showContacts can also be included in the query string with a value of "no". The request does not contain a request body.

### Response

The response to the request has a status code of 200 and returns a JSON object. The object includes a status field and an array of groups, where each group has an id, name, company_id, deleted_at, created_at, and updated_at fields.

# Get Contacts

This endpoint retrieves the list of contacts.

## Request

### Endpoint

`GET` {{api_endpoint}}api/wpbox/getContacts

### Query Parameters

- `token` (string, required): The authentication token.
    

## Response

- `status` (string): The status of the response.
    
- `contacts` (array): An array of contact objects with the following properties:
    - `id` (integer): The unique identifier of the contact.
        
    - `name` (string): The name of the contact.
        
    - `phone` (string): The phone number of the contact.
        
    - `avatar` (string): The avatar URL of the contact.
        
    - `country_id` (integer): The ID of the country associated with the contact.
        
    - `company_id` (integer): The ID of the company associated with the contact.
        
    - `deleted_at` (string): The date and time when the contact was deleted, if applicable.
        
    - `created_at` (string): The date and time when the contact was created.
        
    - `updated_at` (string): The date and time when the contact was last updated.
        
    - `last_reply_at` (string): The date and time of the last reply from the contact.
        
    - `last_client_reply_at` (string): The date and time of the last reply from the client.
        
    - `last_support_reply_at` (string): The date and time of the last reply from the support team.
        
    - `last_message` (string): The last message sent by the contact.
        
    - `is_last_message_by_contact` (integer): Indicates if the last message was sent by the contact (1) or not (0).
        
    - `has_chat` (integer): Indicates if the contact has an active chat (1) or not (0).
        
    - `resolved_chat` (integer): Indicates if the chat with the contact is resolved (1) or not (0).
        
    - `user_id` (integer): The ID of the user associated with the contact, if applicable.
        
    - `enabled_ai_bot` (integer): Indicates if the AI bot is enabled for the contact (1) or not (0).
        

Example:

``` json
{
    "status": "",
    "contacts": [
        {
            "id": 0,
            "name": "",
            "phone": "",
            "avatar": "",
            "country_id": 0,
            "company_id": 0,
            "deleted_at": null,
            "created_at": "",
            "updated_at": "",
            "last_reply_at": "",
            "last_client_reply_at": null,
            "last_support_reply_at": "",
            "last_message": "",
            "is_last_message_by_contact": 0,
            "has_chat": 0,
            "resolved_chat": 0,
            "user_id": null,
            "enabled_ai_bot": 0
        }
    ]
}

 ```

# Get Contact

This endpoint retrieves data for single contact

## Request

### Endpoint

`GET` {{api_endpoint}}api/wpbox/getSingleContact

### Query Parameters

- `token` (string, required): The authentication token.
    
- phone ( string, optional if contact_id is there )
    
- contact_d ( string, optional if phone_id is there )
    

## Response

- `status` (string): The status of the response.
    
- `contact` (array): Contact objects with

### Make Contact

This endpoint allows you to make contact with a user.

- The request should be sent to `{{api_endpoint}}api/wpbox/makeContact` using the HTTP POST method.
    

#### Request Body

The request body should be in the raw format and include the following parameters:

- `token` (string): The token for authentication.
    
- `phone` (string): The phone number of the contact.
    
- `name` (string): The name of the contact. _Optional_
    
- `groups` (string): Comma separated list of groups. _Optional_
    
- `custom` (object): The custom fields of the contact. _Optional_
    

#### Response

- Status: 200 OK
    
- `status` (string): The status of the contact request.
    
- `contact` (object): Details of the contact including id, name, phone, avatar, country_id, company_id, deleted_at, created_at, updated_at, last_reply_at, last_client_reply_at, last_support_reply_at, last_message, is_last_message_by_contact, has_chat, resolved_chat, user_id, and enabled_ai_bot.

request.description:
### Send Message to WhatsApp On Cloud API

This endpoint allows you to send a custom message to a phone number.

Use this API if you are sure client has responded / send you message in less than 24 hours.

---

**Request Body**

- `token` (string) - The token for authentication.
    
- `phone` (string) - The phone number of the recipient.
    
- `message` (string) - The message to be sent
    
- `buttons` (array) - List of buttons
    
- `header` (string) - Header - only applied if there are buttons
    
- `footer` (string) - Header - only applied if there are buttons
    

---

**Response**

- `status` (string) - The status of the message delivery.
    
- `message_id` (integer) - The ID of the sent message.
    
- `message_wamid` (string) - The WhatsApp message ID.
    

_Note: A status code of 200 indicates a successful request._

exec: pm.test("Response has the required fields", function () {
pm.test("Status field must be a non-empty string", function () {
pm.test("Message ID is a non-negative integer", function () {
pm.test("Message_wamid is a non-empty string", function () {

### Make Contact

This endpoint allows you to make contact with a user.

- The request should be sent to `{{api_endpoint}}api/wpbox/makeContact` using the HTTP POST method.
    

#### Request Body

The request body should be in the raw format and include the following parameters:

- `token` (string): The token for authentication.
- `phone` (string): The phone number of the contact.
    

#### Response

- Status: 200 OK
- `status` (string): The status of the contact request.
- `contact` (object): Details of the contact including id, name, phone, avatar, country_id, company_id, deleted_at, created_at, updated_at, last_reply_at, last_client_reply_at, last_support_reply_at, last_message, is_last_message_by_contact, has_chat, resolved_chat, user_id, and enabled_ai_bot.
